testCases.title: The user should be able to register successfully when valid email and password are provided
preconditions: User is on the Sign Up screen
Email address is not already registered

steps.content: Enter a unique, valid email address (e.g., 'newuser@example.com') in the email field
steps.expected: Email field accepts the input and no inline validation error is displayed

steps.content: Enter a strong password (e.g., 'StrongPass123!') in the password field that meets assumed complexity requirements (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special char)
steps.expected: Password field accepts the input, masks characters, and inline validation indicates strong password strength
User is successfully registered and redirected to the post-registration landing page or logged in state

testCases.expectedResult: User account is created and user is authenticated.

testCases.title: The registration form should prevent registration when an email address is already registered
preconditions: User is on the Sign Up screen
An account with 'existing@example.com' already exists

steps.expected: Email field accepts the input

steps.content: Enter a strong password (e.g., 'StrongPass123!') in the password field
steps.expected: Password field accepts the input and masks characters
An error message 'This email is already registered. Please sign in or use a different email.' is displayed. User remains on the Sign Up screen.

testCases.expectedResult: User is unable to register with an existing email address.

testCases.title: The 'Sign Up' button should be disabled when required email or password fields are empty
preconditions: User is on the Sign Up screen

steps.content: Ensure both email and password fields are empty
steps.expected: The 'Sign Up' button is disabled

steps.content: Enter a valid email (e.g., 'test@example.com') in the email field, leave password empty
steps.expected: The 'Sign Up' button remains disabled

steps.content: Clear the email field, enter a valid password (e.g., 'Password123!') in the password field
steps.expected: The 'Sign Up' button remains disabled

testCases.expectedResult: The 'Sign Up' button remains disabled until both email and password fields contain valid input.

testCases.title: The user should be able to log in successfully when valid email and password are provided
preconditions: User is on the Sign In screen
An account with 'valid@example.com' and password 'ValidPass123!' exists

steps.expected: Email field accepts the input
Password field accepts the input and masks characters
User is successfully logged in and redirected to the post-login landing page

testCases.expectedResult: User is authenticated and gains access to the application.

testCases.title: The login form should display an error message when a non-existent email is entered
preconditions: User is on the Sign In screen

steps.expected: Email field accepts the input

steps.content: Enter any password (e.g., 'AnyPass123!') in the password field
steps.expected: Password field accepts the input and masks characters
An error message 'Invalid email or password.' is displayed. User remains on the Sign In screen.

testCases.expectedResult: User is prevented from logging in with a non-existent email and receives an appropriate error.

testCases.title: The login form should display an error message when an incorrect password is entered for an existing account
preconditions: User is on the Sign In screen
An account with 'valid@example.com' and password 'ValidPass123!' exists

steps.expected: Email field accepts the input

steps.content: Enter an incorrect password (e.g., 'WrongPass123!') in the password field
steps.expected: Password field accepts the input and masks characters
An error message 'Invalid email or password.' is displayed. User remains on the Sign In screen.

testCases.expectedResult: User is prevented from logging in with an incorrect password and receives an appropriate error.

testCases.title: The 'Sign In' button should be disabled when required email or password fields are empty
preconditions: User is on the Sign In screen

steps.content: Ensure both email and password fields are empty
steps.expected: The 'Sign In' button is disabled

steps.content: Enter a valid email (e.g., 'test@example.com') in the email field, leave password empty
steps.expected: The 'Sign In' button remains disabled

steps.content: Clear the email field, enter a valid password (e.g., 'Password123!') in the password field
steps.expected: The 'Sign In' button remains disabled

testCases.expectedResult: The 'Sign In' button remains disabled until both email and password fields contain valid input.

testCases.title: The user should be able to log in or register successfully using Google SSO
preconditions: User is on the Sign In/Sign Up screen
User has a valid Google account

steps.content: Click the 'Sign in with Google' button (displaying recognizable brand logo)
steps.expected: A Google authentication pop-up or redirect is initiated

steps.content: Select or enter valid Google account credentials in the Google authentication flow
steps.expected: Google successfully authenticates the user

steps.content: Grant necessary permissions if prompted by Google
steps.expected: Permissions are granted and the flow continues

steps.content: Observe the application's behavior after Google authentication
steps.expected: User is successfully logged in or registered (if new) and redirected to the post-login/registration landing page

testCases.expectedResult: User is authenticated via Google SSO and gains access to the application.

testCases.title: The user should remain on the login/registration screen when Google SSO flow is cancelled
preconditions: User is on the Sign In/Sign Up screen

steps.content: Click the 'Sign in with Google' button
steps.expected: A Google authentication pop-up or redirect is initiated

steps.content: Cancel the Google authentication flow (e.g., close the pop-up, click 'Back')
steps.expected: The Google authentication flow is terminated
User remains on the original Sign In/Sign Up screen without being logged in or registered

testCases.expectedResult: Google SSO cancellation does not alter the user's state on the login/registration screen.

testCases.title: The user should be able to successfully reset their password via the 'Forgot Password' flow
preconditions: User is on the Sign In screen or Forgot Password screen
An account with 'reset@example.com' exists

steps.expected: User is navigated to the 'Forgot Password' screen
Email field accepts the input
A confirmation message 'Password reset link sent to your email.' is displayed, and an email is sent to 'reset@example.com'

steps.content: Open the email and click the password reset link
steps.expected: User is navigated to the 'Reset Password' page with fields for new password

steps.content: Enter a new strong password (e.g., 'NewStrongPass123!') in both 'New Password' and 'Confirm Password' fields
steps.expected: Both fields accept input and inline validation indicates strong password strength
A success message 'Your password has been reset successfully.' is displayed, and user is redirected to the Sign In screen

testCases.expectedResult: User's password is successfully reset, and they can log in with the new password.

testCases.title: The 'Forgot Password' flow should inform the user when an email address is not registered
preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password screen

steps.expected: Email field accepts the input
A message indicating that the email is not found or that a reset link has been sent (to avoid user enumeration) is displayed. No email is sent to 'unknown@example.com'.

testCases.expectedResult: The system handles requests for non-existent emails gracefully without revealing user existence.

testCases.title: The password reset link should be invalid after it has expired
preconditions: User has requested a password reset for 'expired@example.com'
The generated reset link has expired (e.g., after 15 minutes)

steps.content: Click the expired password reset link from the email
steps.expected: User is navigated to a page indicating the link is expired, with an option to request a new link

testCases.expectedResult: Expired password reset links are not usable and prompt the user to request a new one.

testCases.title: The password reset link should be invalid if it is malformed or incorrect
preconditions: User is on the Forgot Password screen

steps.content: Manually navigate to a malformed password reset URL (e.g., change characters in the token)
steps.expected: User is navigated to an error page indicating an invalid link, or redirected to the Forgot Password screen with an error message

testCases.expectedResult: Malformed or incorrect password reset links are rejected by the system.

testCases.title: The email field should display inline validation error for invalid email formats
preconditions: User is on the Sign In or Sign Up screen

steps.expected: Inline error message 'Please enter a valid email address' is displayed below the email field
Inline error message 'Please enter a valid email address' is displayed below the email field
Inline error message 'Please enter a valid email address' is displayed below the email field
Inline error message 'Please enter a valid email address' is displayed below the email field
Inline error message 'Please enter a valid email address' is displayed below the email field
The inline error message disappears, and the email field is marked as valid

testCases.expectedResult: Real-time inline validation correctly identifies and provides feedback for invalid email formats.

testCases.title: The password field should display inline feedback for password strength and requirements
preconditions: User is on the Sign Up screen

steps.expected: Inline feedback (e.g., 'Password is too short') and/or strength indicator (e.g., 'Weak') is displayed. Contextual assistance for password requirements (e.g., 'Minimum 8 characters') is visible.

Inline feedback updates (e.g., 'Needs uppercase letter, number, special character') and/or strength indicator updates (e.g., 'Medium'). Contextual assistance updates to reflect remaining requirements.
Inline feedback updates (e.g., 'Needs uppercase letter, special character') and/or strength indicator updates. Contextual assistance updates.

steps.content: Append 'A!' to make it 'shortpassword1A!' (assuming this meets all requirements)
steps.expected: Inline feedback indicates 'Strong password' or similar. All contextual assistance requirements disappear or are marked as met.

testCases.expectedResult: Real-time inline feedback and contextual assistance for password strength and requirements are displayed and updated dynamically.

testCases.title: The input fields should handle very long email addresses and passwords gracefully
preconditions: User is on the Sign Up screen

steps.content: Enter an extremely long, valid email address (e.g., 250+ characters, like 'verylongemailaddresswithmanycharactersandsubdomains@subdomain.anotherdomain.co.uk')
steps.expected: The email field accepts the input without breaking UI layout. If there's a character limit, an inline error is displayed.
